titleInfo.title: Promovere Incrementum Culturae Catholicae: An International Journal of the Centre for the Study of Christian Culture

name.namePart: 東京純心女子大学キリスト教文化研究センター
Tōkyō junshin joshi daigaku Kirisutokyō bunka kenkyū sentā
Tōkyō junshin joshi daigaku Kirisutokyō bunka kenkyū sentā
東京純心女子大学キリスト教文化研究センター

mods.note: Since there is no "th"-sound in Japanese, the reading of θο (tho) is sometimes "to ト", sometimes "so ソ". This trend may also have been affected by the English way of pronouncing "th", that is influential in shaping todays Japanese vocabulary. According to the authors and depending on the time, "(the) Catholic Church" was written either Katorikku kyōkai カトリック教会 or Kasorikku kyōkai カソリック教会, even though today the former is more common. Θεσσαλονίκη (Thessaloniki) is often written "Tessaroniki テッサロニキ", but sometimes there also is the spelling "Sesaroniki セサロニキ". On the homepage of the Christian Center the given reading is: "Katorikosu カトリコス". See: https://www.t-junshin.ac.jp/univ/cla/ChristianCenter/, therefore we have this as the preferred reference.
